strands.title: Schema completion and base model discovery: Pydantic v2 foundation
strands.content: Cycles 35-36 establish that Apacheta's immutable Pydantic v2 foundation (frozen=True, extra='forbid', ser_json_bytes='base64', validate_default=True) enforces append-only history by design. Every TensorRecord is immutable; updates produce new instances, creating durable lineage via predecessors_in_scope pointers. This architectural substrate is not theoretical—it is the concrete enforcer of the projection algorithm's correctness guarantees. Serialization via model_dump/model_validate enables JSON persistence and IPC, making checkpointing and long-context handoff feasible. The immutable schema is the foundation upon which the entire projector rests. Cycle 37 adds no new content on this strand—it remains established and validated. The foundation is sound; focus shifts to real-world integration.

key_claims.text: ApachetaBaseModel enforces frozen=True (immutability), extra='forbid' (strict schema), ser_json_bytes='base64' (binary handling), validate_default=True (validated defaults).
Frozen Pydantic models cannot be mutated in-place; all updates produce new instances. This enforces immutable-append history by design.
Immutability at the schema level guarantees: prior tensors are never corrupted, history is append-only, predecessors_in_scope links are durable.
The five-phase projection algorithm must assemble a COMPLETE new TensorRecord before validation; partial updates are impossible within the Pydantic model design.
model_dump/model_validate enable TensorRecord serialization to/from JSON, enabling persistence, IPC, and checkpoint/restore workflows.

strands.title: Minimal viable projection (MVP) specification: strands, claims, losses, branch prediction, open questions
strands.content: Cycles 35-36 converge on MVP scope: semantic content (strands, claims with T/I/F, declared losses, instructions_for_next, open_questions) vs. deferred infrastructure (provenance, composition equations, advanced epistemic metadata). The MVP targets H0 validation (projection maintains coherence better than append-only) with minimum necessary complexity. Cycle 36 adds a critical insight: **declared losses may be drift-reduction mechanism**. If a projector explicitly declares 'I dropped thread X because of Y,' the next projection can consciously re-acquire or leave dropped. Silent loss is where drift begins—the model forgets what it forgot. Making losses explicit transforms them from a hazard into a correction signal. This elevates declared losses from honesty mechanism to active functional component of coherence maintenance. Cycle 37 inherits this specification unchanged—no new requirements emerge. The MVP scope remains stable and implementable.

key_claims.text: MVP focuses on semantic content (strands, claims, losses, branch prediction, open questions) vs. infrastructure (provenance, composition equations, advanced epistemic metadata).
Strands are the semantic backbone: thematic threads that integrate prior and new content, compressing reasoning trajectory into structure.
Key claims with T/I/F are the knowledge units. Projection updates T/I/F based on evidence, explicitly states contradictions and convergences.
Declared losses (category, rationale) are the honesty mechanism. Silence about incompleteness is the enemy; declared losses are the antidote.
Declared losses may function as drift-reduction mechanism: explicit knowledge of what was dropped enables next projection to consciously re-acquire or leave dropped.
Instructions_for_next operationalizes the preservation_target: what should the next cycle focus on? This is branch prediction for long-context coherence.
MVP deferral of provenance and composition equations is justified: they are bookkeeping and scaling infrastructure, not core to H0 validation.

strands.title: Real-world validation infrastructure: conversation log structure and data landscape
strands.content: Cycle 36 planned real-world validation using conversation logs in /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/. Cycle 37 explores data landscape: 15 experiment directories (baseline_run1/2/3, cognitive_transactions, comparison_*, full_optimization_run1, long_compact_trim_run1, minimal_sysprompt_run1/2, test_author_run1/2/3, verify_streaming_fix_run1) discovered via bash. Each experiment contains: config.json (configuration), git_log.txt (version history), logs/ (conversation data), session/ (session state), test_results.txt (validation output). This structure suggests experiments are LLM conversation runs with configurable parameters, outputs, and test results. **Data accessibility confirmed**: real conversation logs are present and structured. **Immediate discovery needed**: examine logs/ directory to understand turn-by-turn format, message structure, metadata availability. This is critical for PHASE 1 (semantic parsing) and PHASE 2 (embedding generation) specification. Cycle 37 enters data exploration phase: understanding format informs embedding strategy, strand parsing heuristics, and validation measurement alignment.

key_claims.text: 15 experiment directories found in /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/. Experiments appear to be LLM conversation runs with varying parameters and optimization strategies.
Each experiment has consistent structure: config.json, git_log.txt, logs/, session/, test_results.txt. Suggests standardized experimental framework.
logs/ directory is the target for projector validation. Format and structure unknown but accessible. Next action: explore logs/ to understand turn/message structure.
Data landscape supports real-world validation as designed in cycle 36. No blocking issues detected so far. Data exploration continues.

strands.title: Projected projector architecture: standalone module, Haiku integration point, testable semantics
strands.content: Cycle 35 established projector as a pure function (input: tensor_d + transformer_output; output: tensor_d'). Cycle 36 sketched five-phase architecture (parse → align → update → declare → finalize) and pivoted to real-world validation. Cycle 37 maintains architecture unchanged; adds concrete execution readiness: (1) **Data landscape is confirmed accessible**. 15 experiment directories with structured format enable immediate data exploration and projector testing. (2) **PHASE 1/2 specification (heuristic + Sentence Transformers + 0.65 threshold) remains proposed** pending examination of actual log format. Once format understood, confirmation or adjustment follows immediately. (3) **Validation measurement deferral ratified by cycle 37 progress**: understanding log structure will inform metric choice. Embedding dispersion metric is natural if logs contain structured text; drift rate metric if logs track claim changes; carry-forward metric if logs show topic progression. (4) **Integration point remains clear**: projector is called at end of each conversation turn or cycle, reads prior TensorRecord, receives new conversation content, produces updated TensorRecord. Modularity intact. (5) **Testability confirmed**: isolated projector function can be tested on real data immediately. No external dependencies discovered yet except embedding model availability (pending confirmation).

key_claims.text: Projector is a memory controller (separate from reasoning ALU). Input: (tensor_d, transformer_output). Output: tensor_d'. Pure function, deterministic.

Separation enables testability (projector tested in isolation), swappability (compare projection strategies), auditability (deterministic transformations), integration (Haiku calls projector at cycle end).
Five-phase projector API (parse → align → update → declare → finalize) remains the implementation roadmap. Format confirmation pending data exploration.
Projector input/output is fully Pydantic-based: reads TensorRecord or dict, produces new frozen TensorRecord. JSON serializable end-to-end.
Real data landscape (15 experiments, structured directories) confirms feasibility of real-world testing path chosen in cycle 36.

strands.title: Cycle 37 execution state: data exploration initiated, format discovery pending, validation path locked
strands.content: Cycle 36 completed: specification, architecture, pivot to real-world validation. Cycle 37 begins execution phase: **(A) Data landscape exploration**: 15 experiments discovered, directory structure confirmed, data accessibility validated. Logs are present and structured. (B) **Next immediate action**: examine logs/ directory from one baseline experiment (e.g., baseline_run1/logs/) to understand: turn/message format, metadata availability, conversation length, text content richness. This examination is the critical gate for PHASE 1/2 specification finalization. (C) **Format discovery will unlock**: (1) Parsing strategy (heuristic sufficient? Or need LLM?). (2) Embedding generation approach (can strands be formed from turn-level content? Or need finer granularity?). (3) Validation metric selection (dispersion, drift, carry-forward, or hybrid?). (D) **Validation path is locked**: real-world data is confirmed. Toy validation (cycle 28) status remains unknown and is now superseded. Cycle 37 does not wait for toy validation; it proceeds with real data. (E) **Implementation clock starts**: cycle 37 is exploration + early implementation. Phase 1 (semantic parsing) skeleton code can begin in parallel with format discovery. By end of cycle 37, format should be understood and Phase 1/2 specification should be finalized. (F) **H0 validation target**: by end of cycle 37 or early cycle 38, project a real conversation (20-50 turns) and measure coherence. This is the critical experiment.

key_claims.text: Cycle 37 transitions from specification (cycles 35-36) to execution (cycles 37+). Data landscape is confirmed accessible. Format discovery is the immediate blocker.
15 experiment directories with 'logs/', 'config.json', 'test_results.txt' structure confirmed. Format inspection will inform all downstream decisions.
Real-world validation path is locked. Toy validation (cycle 28) is superseded. No wait for unknown results; proceed with accessible data.
Phase 1/2 specification will be finalized after format discovery. Heuristic + Sentence Transformers + 0.65 threshold remains the proposal pending confirmation.
Implementation clock starts cycle 37. Phase 1 skeleton code can begin in parallel with format exploration. H0 validation target: project real conversation by end of cycle 37 or early cycle 38.

strands.title: User hypothesis: declared losses as active drift-reduction mechanism
strands.content: Cycle 36 introduces hypothesis that declared losses (category, rationale) enable downstream projections to consciously re-acquire dropped threads or leave them dropped. The insight: **silent loss is where drift starts**. When content is dropped without acknowledgment, next projection cannot recover or re-acquire. But declared losses provide signal (uncertainty, irrelevance, context pressure). Cycle 37 inherits this hypothesis unchanged. It will be tested empirically once projector is applied to real conversations. The mechanism will become clearer during implementation: (A) Does PHASE 3 evidence counting naturally identify high-uncertainty claims? (B) Do these claims map to content that later gets dropped? (C) Do downstream projections recognize and re-acquire declared losses? H1 (declared losses improve coherence) is secondary to H0 (projection beats append-only) but is empirically testable and may explain the mechanism if H0 is confirmed. Cycle 37 does not add new theoretical content; awaits implementation and validation.

key_claims.text: Silent loss is where drift starts: when content is dropped without acknowledgment, next projection cannot recover or consciously re-acquire.
Declared losses enable downstream projection to recognize dropped thread reason and consciously decide: re-acquire or leave dropped.
Declared losses signal uncertainty: high-uncertainty claims often dropped first. This provides signal for next projection to focus on weak points.
Losses as feature: transforms loss from shame ('I lost track') to signal ('Here's my uncertainty boundary'). Operationalizes drift reduction.
H1 hypothesis: 'Declared losses improve coherence by enabling conscious re-acquisition and drift signal.' Empirically testable once projector is applied to real data.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed specification of logs/ directory format. Cycle 37 discovers logs/ exist but does not inspect contents. Format remains unknown.
declared_losses.why: Format inspection is deferred to next immediate action (bash ls logs/ or head logs/*). Critical for parsing strategy but not yet executed. Deferring keeps projection focused on state after cycle 37 user interaction.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specification of PHASE 1 (semantic parsing) based on actual log format. Heuristic strategy remains proposed but not confirmed.
declared_losses.why: Confirmation requires knowing log structure (JSON? Plain text? Structured fields?). This confirmation cannot happen until format is inspected. Deferring to post-exploration cycle is pragmatic.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Final embedding model confirmation. Sentence Transformers MiniLM remains proposed; availability in Yanantin environment not yet confirmed.
declared_losses.why: Model availability check deferred to implementation setup. Proposal is sound; confirmation is pragmatic bookkeeping. Can be done in parallel with format exploration.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Validation metric finalization. Cycle 36 deferred to post-exploration. Cycle 37 discovers data landscape but does not select metric.
declared_losses.why: Metric selection should follow format discovery. Understanding whether logs contain structured metadata (for drift rate) or plain text (for embedding dispersion) will inform choice. Deferring is justified.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details on how the 15 experiments differ. Names suggest variations (baseline vs. optimization, different prompts, streaming fixes) but specifics not explored.
declared_losses.why: Experiment variation is less critical than understanding format of individual logs. Can be explored after projector is working. Focus remains on getting to first working validation.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Investigation of config.json and test_results.txt in baseline experiments. Could reveal experiment parameters, expected outputs, prior validation attempts.
declared_losses.why: These are secondary sources. Primary target is logs/ directory structure. Format discovery is the critical path. Config exploration can follow.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Codebase search for existing projection algorithm (deferred from cycle 36). Still not executed.
declared_losses.why: Real data access and immediate implementation path make codebase search lower priority. Can be revisited if duplicate work is detected. Pragmatic deferral.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: H1 hypothesis refinement. Declared losses as drift-reduction remains stated but awaits implementation evidence.
declared_losses.why: H1 is secondary to H0. Cannot be validated without projector running on real data. Theoretical content is complete; empirical validation deferred to cycle 37-38.

open_questions: Data format in logs/ directory: what is conversation log structure? JSON per turn? Single log file? Plain text? Structured fields (metadata, timestamps, role labels)?
Parsing strategy for PHASE 1: once format is known, is heuristic tokenization sufficient? Or does actual format demand LLM-based semantic parsing?
Embedding generation: should embeddings be at turn level, strand level, or claim level? How granular?
How should evidence weighting in PHASE 3 handle contradictory evidence? If claim has both supporting and opposing mentions, what is epistemic update rule?
What is the appropriate similarity threshold (PHASE 2) for real conversation data? 0.65 was proposed for toy tasks; may need calibration for real text.
Should very old strands (e.g., from early conversation cycles) be archived, merged, or carried forward indefinitely? Affects tensor size growth.
H1 (declared losses as drift-reduction): can this hypothesis be validated empirically? How to distinguish mechanism from confound?
How will projector be applied to real logs? One tensor per conversation? One tensor per turn? Hierarchical projection (local tensors + global tensor)?
Embedding model: is Sentence Transformers MiniLM available in Yanantin environment? Inference latency for embedding (100, 1000, 10000 strands)?
Validation sample size: how many real conversations (or turns) are needed for statistically significant coherence measurement?
Can embedding dispersion metric be computed efficiently? What clustering algorithm? K-means? Hierarchical? DBSCAN?
How does projector handle conversation context shifts (e.g., topic change from technical to personal)? Should strands adapt or stabilize?
Are there existing metrics in NLP for 'discourse coherence'? Can we use established measures instead of designing from scratch?
How will autoresearch optimize across hyperparameters? Grid search? Bayesian optimization? Multi-objective (dispersion + computation cost)?
Which baseline_run (1, 2, 3) should be used for first validation? Or should all three be tested for robustness?
